statement.en: Differentially Private De-identification of Dutch Clinical Notes: A Comparative Evaluation
statement.summary: Protecting patient privacy in clinical narratives is essential for enabling secondary use of healthcare data under regulations such as GDPR and HIPAA. While manual de-identification remains the gold standard, it is costly and slow, motivating the need for automated methods that combine privacy guarantees with high utility. Most automated text de-identification pipelines employed named entity recognition (NER) to identify protected entities for redaction. Although methods based on differential privacy (DP) provide formal privacy guarantees, more recently also large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for text de-identification in the clinical domain. In this work, we present the first comparative study of DP, NER, and LLMs for Dutch clinical text de-identification. We investigate t

proposer: Michele Miranda, Xinlan Yan, Nishant Mishra et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper is dated 2026-04-25 (future publication), explicitly addresses comparative validation of three de-identification paradigms (DP, NER, LLMs) on Dutch clinical notes. Claims 'first comparative study' suggest novel empirical contribution under active development. No established consensus exists on relative performance trade-offs across these methods for non-English clinical corpora. Status is provisional pending peer review and reproduction.
